title: ulistdel
text: ulistdel Delete an item from an item list. Explain this code long ulistdel (UHACThList, intoption, intindex, UCHAR*Id); Copy Parameters hList Handle to the list, as returned by ulistnew option Yype of item to be deleted. Possible values are: UITEM_OPTION_NONE —deletes an indexed item. You must supply a value for index ; the value for Id is ignored. UITEM_OPTION_ID —deletes an associative item. You must supply a value for Id ; the value for index is ignored. UITEM_OPTION_ID_CASE —as UITEM_OPTION_ID , except that the Id is handled in a case-sensitive manner (for example, deleting an item with Id " a " does not delete an item with Id " A "). index Index of the item, if the item is indexed Id ID of the item, if the item is associative Return Values Table 1. Values returned by ulistdel Value Meaning 0 Success. -165 The supplied handle hList is not valid. -1112 The value of option is not valid. -1203 The value of id is out of range. -1406 Memory could not be allocated or freed. Use Library: ucall Description ulistdel deletes an item from the specified list. This function is very similar to the ProcScript command delitem . The variable option determines if the item is indexed or associative. Note that there is no safeguard if you do not provide the expected parameters. For example, if you use UITEM_OPTION_NONE but fail to supply a value for Id , your program is likely to crash. It is allowed, although not recommended, to mix indexed and associative items in one list. For example, you can remove an item by its index from a list that contains only associative items (but not the other way around). Example: ulistnew, ulist2uf, ulistdel, and ulistput The following example creates an associative list, consisting of country codes and names, and passes it to a service, which prints it to the message frame. The operation DOLIST in service TESTSVC is defined as follows: Explain this code operation dolist params string thelist : in; endparams putmess "%%thelist" end Copy The 3GL code that creates the list and passes it to service DOLIST is as follows: Explain this code char *countries[] = { "NL", "Netherlands", "GB", "Great Britain", "D", "Germany", "F", "France", "?", "huh?", "I", "Italy", "E", "Spain", 0 }; #include <h3gl.h> void pass_itemlist () { UHACT hEnv, hIn; /* Environment and instance handle */ UHACT hOp; /* Operation handles for OP1 amd OP2 */ long actStatus, procErr; /* Activation status and procerr */ UHACT hList; /* Item list handle */ int i; /* Loop variable */ ueopen(&hEnv); /* Get environment handle */ uinstnew (hEnv, (UCHAR *)"TESTSVC", 0, 0, UDEFAULT_COMM_MODE, 0, &hIn, &procErr);/* Instantiate service TESTSVC */ uinstopr (hIn, (UCHAR *)"DOLIST", 0, &hOp);/* Instantiate operation DOLIST*/ ulistnew(hEnv, &hList); /* Create an item list */ for ( i=0; countries[i]; i+= 2 ) /* Loop through the data */ { ulistput(hList, UITEM_OPTION_ID, 0, countries[i], countries[i+1] ); /* Add the list item */ } ulistdel(hList, UITEM_OPTION_ID, 0, "?");/* Delete the item with id "?" */ ulist2uf(hOp, 1, hList); /* Copy item list to operation param*/ uopract(hOp, &actStatus, &procErr); /* Activate the operation */ uinstdel (hIn, 0); /* Destroy instance */ ufreeh (&hList); /* Free list handle */ ufreeh (&hOp); /* Free operation handle */ ufreeh (&hIn); /* Free instance handle */ ufreeh (&hEnv); /* Free environment handle */ } Copy The operation DOLIST prints this line to the message frame: Explain this code NL=Netherlands;GB=Great Britain;D=Germany;F=France;I=Italy;E=Spain Copy Related Links ulistput Insert an item into an item list. delitem Deletes an item from a list.
